pulls.title: Add tokentype
pulls.author: smhendrickson
pulls.body:
From the the protocol doc, section 6 [1]

[1]: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-privacypass-protocol-08#name-issuance-protocol-for-publi

comments.body:
```suggestion
- Public Metadata: Arbitrary length metadata that can be viewed by the Client, Attester, Issuer, and Origin. Public metadata is bound Tokens issued by the issuer and therefore cannot be changed.
```

```suggestion
{{Section 6 of PROTOCOL}} in that the issuance and redemption protocols carry metadata provided by the Client and visible to the Attester, Issuer, and Origin. This means Clients can set arbitrary metadata when requesting a token, but specific values of metadata may be rejected by either Attester, Issuer, or Origin. Similar to a token nonce, metadata is cryptographically bound to a token and cannot be altered.
```
I think we may want to include key generation details in this document, in particular because the PBRSA variant requires a very specific type of key.

```suggestion
- Metadata value: `metadata`, an opaque byte string of length at most 2<sup>16-1</sup> bytes.
```

```suggestion
this protocol are described below. The constant `Nk` is defined as 256 for token type 0x1234 in
```

```suggestion
token_input = concat(0x1234, // Token type field is 2 bytes long
                     len_in_bytes(metadata), // 2-byte length of metadata
                     metadata,
```

This is so that the signature covers everything preceding the authenticator in Token. That said, it's redundant... since `Blind` will concatenate the input to the metadata, and could therefore be more expensive than desired.
The current draft requires PrepareRandomize, not PrepareIdentity, so this may need some work.
Blind also returns the randomized prefix that's appended to the message.
What does "that all blinded_msg values are encoded for" mean?

I would drop "satisfies issuer requirements" since it's not clear what that means. I would instead lean on text elsewhere that says something like "metadata values are defined by the deployment and have privacy considerations; see <architecture doc> for more details."
Since this is a new TokenRequest structure, we need a new media type.
We'd also need a new media type for this, too.
This new Token type will need a new media type, too. I wonder if we should name these something else so there's no confusion between these types and the existing ones.
👍 Yeah, this looks like it still needs to be updated.
I assume this key generation advice has to be updated still?
